title: Compact gate heuristic that overstates evidential hardness on first read
scenario: `sol-cross-030` presents a compact gate framework that sounds operational and disciplined, but the compactness itself can make the heuristic look materially stronger than the underlying evidence actually is.
signals_for_escalation: gate language makes the card sound harder and more settled than a loose proposal
formal compactness can be misread as evidence density
the framework shape invites over-reading as a general scoring or review pressure signal
signals_against_escalation: the card remains heuristic and context-specific rather than repeatedly validated
compact structure does not create new material proof by itself
the current reading problem is interpretive discipline, not a new unresolved scoring emergency
why_no_material_escalation: The case does not need a new escalation because the main risk lies in over-reading formal compactness, not in a genuinely unresolved scoring defect or new exception pressure.
why_not_s2_entry_relevant: A compact gate heuristic does not reveal a missing S2 axis. It remains a disciplined proposal pattern that current review can already read without expanding the scoring model.
why_not_adjacent_escalation_readings: This is not a fresh soft-flag-worthy escalation because the issue is not an unresolved active score spike. It is not a justified exception because no narrow bounded proof surface is being defended. It is not a true S2-near case because formal compactness alone does not establish a missing interpretive layer.
notes: Use this case to stop gate-shaped proposals from being mistaken for materially harder evidence than they contain.
